claim_bullets.bullet_text: Functions in JavaScript are values that represent computations, not just numbers or strings (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-43).
Applying a function to zero or more arguments produces a value, just as 2 + 2 yields 4 (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-43).
The simplest possible function is `() => 0`, which returns 0 when applied (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-43).
Evaluating the same function expression repeatedly creates new, distinct functions, illustrating that functions are reference types (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-43).
In JavaScript, parentheses might be used both to group expressions and to invoke functions, a pattern common in other languages (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-43).
